description: Out-of-generator and low-prevalence stress tests (paper section 'Out-of-Generator and Low-Prevalence Stress Tests' + Appendix 'Held-Out Generator (AASE-B) and Prevalence Protocols'). The trained TrustGuard policy is evaluated FROZEN under distribution shift; the recalibration row re-runs only the Lagrangian dual update for 72 simulated hours at the target prevalence with actor policies frozen. mean over seeds {7, 42, 123, 777, 2024}.

protocol.aase_b: Held-out generator: inter-session-gap bootstrap (resampling gap distributions of a held-out half of sandbox sessions) + self-exciting Hawkes injection lambda(t) = mu0 + sum_{t_j<t} alpha*exp(-beta*(t-t_j)), both fit to the held-out session half.
protocol.low_prevalence: Original AASE generator with malicious fraction reduced to 2% (14/700).
protocol.recalibration: Lagrangian dual update only, 72 simulated hours at target prevalence, actor policies frozen.

key_findings: Under the held-out generator, TrustGuard loses roughly 7.7 AIPR points and drifts marginally past the FRR budget (2.8% vs 2.5%).
At 2% prevalence, per-event coverage remains stable but FRR rises past the budget.
Re-running the dual update at the target prevalence restores feasibility at a coverage cost.
The safety budget is a distribution-conditional property requiring recalibration under shift, not an invariant of the trained policy.
